---
title: "John Robin Inston"
subtitle: "Ph.D. Candidate in Statistics and Applied Probability at UCSB"
image: assets/images/profile-picture.jpeg
title-block-banner: false
listing:
  - id: blog-latest
    contents: posts
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 1
    sort: "date desc"
    max-items: 1
    fields: [title, date, image]
    sort-ui: false
    filter-ui: false
    categories: false
  - id: projects-latest
    contents: projects
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 1
    sort: "date desc"
    max-items: 1
    fields: [title, subtitle]
    sort-ui: false
    filter-ui: false
    categories: false
  - id: teaching-latest
    contents: teaching.qmd
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 1
    max-items: 1
    fields: [title, subtitle, image]
    sort-ui: false
    filter-ui: false
    categories: false
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 14em
  image-shape: rounded
  id: hero-heading
  links:
    - icon: youtube
      aria-label: "YouTube"
      href: https://www.youtube.com/@JohnRobinInston
    - icon: medium
      aria-label: "Medium"
      href: https://medium.com/@johnrobininston
    - icon: linkedin
      aria-label: "LinkedIn"
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/johninston/
    - icon: github
      aria-label: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/johnrobininston

---

<style>
/* Research interest chips */
.chips {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.35rem;
  margin: 0.75rem 0;
}
.chip {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 0.22rem 0.65rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  font-size: 0.8rem;
  font-weight: 500;
  line-height: 1.4;
}
.chip-outline {
  border: 1.5px solid var(--bs-primary, #0d6efd);
  color: var(--bs-primary, #0d6efd);
}

/* Stat highlight row */
.stat-row {
  display: flex;
  gap: 1.5rem;
  margin: 1rem 0 1.1rem;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
}
.stat-item {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  line-height: 1.2;
}
.stat-num {
  font-size: 1.3rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: var(--bs-primary, #0d6efd);
}
.stat-label {
  font-size: 0.75rem;
  color: #888;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
}

/* Areas cards */
#blog-latest .card,
#projects-latest .card,
#teaching-latest .card {
  border: 1px solid rgba(var(--bs-primary-rgb, 13,110,253), 0.25);
  border-radius: 0.6rem;
  box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
  transition: box-shadow 0.2s ease, transform 0.2s ease;
}
#blog-latest .card:hover,
#projects-latest .card:hover,
#teaching-latest .card:hover {
  box-shadow: 0 5px 18px rgba(0,0,0,0.12);
  transform: translateY(-3px);
}
.view-all-link {
  display: inline-block;
  margin-top: 0.6rem;
  font-size: 0.88rem;
  font-weight: 500;
}

/* Vertical timeline */
.timeline {
  position: relative;
  padding-left: 2rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
}
.timeline::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  left: 0.44rem;
  top: 0.45rem;
  bottom: 0.45rem;
  width: 2px;
  background: var(--bs-primary, #0d6efd);
  opacity: 0.22;
}
.tl-item {
  position: relative;
  margin-bottom: 1.6rem;
}
.tl-item::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  left: -1.63rem;
  top: 0.38rem;
  width: 0.58rem;
  height: 0.58rem;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: var(--bs-primary, #0d6efd);
}
.tl-date {
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  color: #888;
  margin-bottom: 0.15rem;
}
.tl-inst {
  font-style: italic;
}
.tl-title {
  font-weight: 600;
  font-size: 1.08rem;
  margin: 0.05rem 0 0.35rem;
}
.tl-body {
  font-size: 1rem;
  color: #555;
}
[data-bs-theme="dark"] .tl-date {
  color: #aaa;
}
[data-bs-theme="dark"] .tl-body {
  color: #bbb;
}
</style>

::: {#hero-heading}
<div class="h1">PhD Candidate &middot; Researcher &middot; Educator</div>

I am a 4th year doctoral candidate in the [Statistics and Applied Probability Department](https://www.pstat.ucsb.edu) at the University of California Santa Barbara. I am a member of the Center for Financial Mathematics and Actuarial Research (CFMAR) and the Simulation and Control in Finance and Insurance (SCiFI) research group under the supervision of [Prof. Michael Ludkovski](https://ludkovski.pstat.ucsb.edu).

My research interests lie in the interface of Financial Mathematics, Stochastic Games, Deep Learning and Groundwater Markets. For my thesis project I am investigating the use of stochastic games to model groundwater markets in California.

<div class="chips">
  <span class="chip chip-outline">Stochastic Games</span>
  <span class="chip chip-outline">Financial Mathematics</span>
  <span class="chip chip-outline">Deep Learning</span>
  <span class="chip chip-outline">Groundwater Markets</span>
</div>

```{=html}
<div class="stat-row">
  <div class="stat-item">
    <span class="stat-num">4th</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Year PhD</span>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-item">
    <span class="stat-num">7</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Courses Taught</span>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-item">
    <span class="stat-num">1</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Working Paper</span>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-item">
    <span class="stat-num">2</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Fellowships</span>
  </div>
</div>
```

I am a recipient of the [Amazon Fellowship](https://www.amazon.science/academic-engagements/amazon-gift-supports-studies-in-responsible-ai-at-university-of-california-santa-barbara) supporting studies in responsible AI and a mentor in the [PALilSaDs program](https://www.paliisads.org/home) which aims to increase degree completion for low-income, high-achieving STEM undergraduates. If you would like to reach out please send me a note using the [contact page](contact.qmd).

<a href="resume.html" class="btn btn-primary mt-2">View Resume</a>

:::

## About me

I am passionate about learning and teaching topics in mathematics, statistics, finance and data science. I am a staunch advocate for open collaboration in academia. I enjoy writing blog posts about topics I find interesting and am committed to sharing my project work and course material.

![Realization of a Gaussian random walk.](assets/images/random-walk.gif)

In addition to the posts on my website I will also be posting the same content to my profile on Medium, as well as (hopefully) publishing content on YouTube aiming to provide advice and helpful content for students in STEM.

## Education Timeline

<div class="timeline">

<div class="tl-item">
<div class="tl-date">2022 — Present &middot; <span class="tl-inst">UC Santa Barbara, California</span></div>
<div class="tl-title">PhD in Statistics and Applied Probability</div>
<div class="tl-body">Advancing to candidacy in 2025 under the supervision of <a href="https://ludkovski.pstat.ucsb.edu">Prof. Mike Ludkovski</a>, <a href="https://fouque.faculty.pstat.ucsb.edu">Prof. Jean-Pierre Fouque</a> and <a href="https://sites.google.com/site/ruimenghu1/">Prof. Ruimeng Hu</a>.</div>
</div>

<div class="tl-item">
<div class="tl-date">2020 — 2021 &middot; <span class="tl-inst">Lancaster University, UK</span></div>
<div class="tl-title">MSc Quantitative Finance <em>(Distinction)</em></div>
<div class="tl-body">Returned to Lancaster during the COVID-19 pandemic. Graduated summa cum laude with the highest GPA and dissertation mark on the programme.</div>
</div>

<div class="tl-item">
<div class="tl-date">2013 — 2018 &middot; <span class="tl-inst">Lancaster University, UK</span></div>
<div class="tl-title">BSc Mathematics with Statistics <em>(First Class Honours)</em></div>
<div class="tl-body">Changed course twice — from Theoretical Physics to Mechanical Engineering and finally to Mathematics with Statistics. Faced significant personal challenges along the way but graduated with a First.</div>
</div>

</div>

## Areas

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}

### Blog

::: {#blog-latest}
:::

<a href="blog.html" class="view-all-link">View all posts →</a>

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}

### Projects

::: {#projects-latest}
:::

<a href="projects.html" class="view-all-link">View all projects →</a>

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}

### Teaching

::: {#teaching-latest}
:::

<a href="teaching.html" class="view-all-link">View all courses →</a>

:::

::::
